content: High levels of estrogen are associated with lower levels of testosterone and higher aromatase activity, which converts androgens into estrogen.
Refined sugar and excess carbohydrates lower sex hormone binding globulin, leading to elevated estrogen levels.
Conventional dairy products contain estrogen and progesterone, suppressing testosterone production according to a Japanese study.
Organic milk or goat's milk is recommended as a healthier alternative to conventional dairy.
Soy contains xenoestrogens that mimic estrogen and can increase estrogen levels in the body.
Cruciferous vegetables, such as broccoli and cauliflower, contain indole-3-carbinol, which reduces estrogen levels in the body.
